comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Confirmed live 2026-07-03 against production Jira REB: acli fetch returns all 628 issues and the real dry-run pass produces a sane 248-update plan with ZERO orphaned-driven actions, yet reconcile-check reports all 627 bindings orphaned. This isolates the defect to reconcile-check's ticket.json loader (the live-acting pass is unaffected). Compiled ticket source confirmed: .cache.json['state'] (keys ticket_id/status/ticket_type/tags/...).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Fix pushed to Gerrit change 267 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/267). Extracted tested load_local_tickets() reading .cache.json['state']; 13 reconcile_check unit tests pass incl. the all-orphaned regression repro; 331 orchestrate+state tests green; lint+typecheck clean. Live re-run post-fix: checked=627, orphaned=0 (was checked=0/orphaned=627). Awaiting LLM-Review + Verified votes.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
`rebar reconcile --mode reconcile-check` (the read-only online drift diagnostic in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/__main__.py`) loads local tickets by reading `<entry>/ticket.json` from each `.tickets-tracker/<id>/` directory. The event-sourced store NEVER writes `ticket.json` — a ticket dir contains event-log files (`*-CREATE.json`, `*-COMMENT.json`, …) plus a compiled `.cache.json` whose `state` key holds the ticket. So `local_tickets` is always empty, and every binding is classified 'bound but missing locally or in Jira'.

## Reproduction Steps
1. On a populated event-sourced store with N confirmed bindings, run `rebar reconcile --mode reconcile-check`.
2. Observe `bridge_state/reconcile-check.json`.

Expected: bindings whose local ticket AND Jira issue both exist are counted checked/in_sync.
Actual: `checked: 0`, `in_sync: 0`, `orphaned_bindings` = all N (e.g. 627/627), regardless of true state. Confirmed live 2026-07-03 against production Jira REB (~628 issues, 627 bindings): acli fetch works (30 active issues returned), yet reconcile-check reported all 627 orphaned.

## Root Cause
`__main__.py` reconcile-check branch: `meta_path = entry / "ticket.json"` — a file the event-sourced store does not produce. `ticket.json` is referenced ONLY here; no other reconciler code reads it. The compiled ticket is `.cache.json['state']` (fields keyed `ticket_id`, `status`, `ticket_type`, `tags`, …), or can be obtained by replaying the event log.

## Impact
The read-only drift diagnostic is unusable — it always reports 100% orphaned drift (false positive). It does NOT affect the live-acting pass (run_pass/the differs load local state through a different, correct path), so no bad mutations result; but any operator relying on reconcile-check for a go/no-go read gets a totally misleading census.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] reconcile-check's local loader reads the compiled ticket from `.cache.json['state']` (or replays the event log) instead of the nonexistent `ticket.json`, keying on `ticket_id`/`status`.
- [ ] On a store where a binding's local ticket and Jira issue both exist, reconcile-check counts it checked/in_sync (not orphaned); a RED unit test in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/` reproduces the all-orphaned bug and passes after the fix.
- [ ] No regression to the live-acting pass (that path is unaffected).
- [ ] Landed on main via Gerrit (LLM-Review + Verified).
compiled_state.title: reconcile-check reads nonexistent per-ticket ticket.json -> falsely reports ALL bindings orphaned
